text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work on code without conflicts. Developers create feature branches to build new features separately from the main codebase. This keeps the main branch stable and clean. When a feature is complete, it can be merged back into the main branch through a pull request. A pull request allows team members to review the changes, suggest improvements, and ensure quality before merging. This workflow improves collaboration and reduces errors. Mastering branching is essential for modern software development.
